codeReview.summary: The reviewed worker session made no code changes because the feature had already landed earlier, and the current implementation still relies on post-commit delete cleanup after hydrate failures instead of keeping batch save within a true commit-boundary atomic region. That violates the feature scope and mission guidance even though the targeted regressions pass.

issues.description: Repo::save_many commits the transaction before hydrating the returned rows, then compensates by deleting parents and tracked foreign children on hydrate failure. This is explicitly post-commit cleanup, not true commit-boundary atomicity, so a later failure still depends on repair after commit rather than the transaction boundary itself.

The same post-commit cleanup pattern remains in Repo::save: hydration happens after COMMIT TRANSACTION, and failures trigger deletes afterward. The reviewed feature description required batch save and retries to depend on the same true commit-boundary atomicity, but the underlying boundary is still not authoritative.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still instructs workers to write failure-path regressions first but does not force them to reject implementations that only make those regressions pass via post-commit deletes strongly enough; this worker stopped at no-op verification even though the code still violated the mission boundary.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript for session ce89d793-f3b9-4c63-b73c-9927fe095236 reports the feature was already implemented and verified without raising the remaining post-commit cleanup issue; current code in core/src/repository/mod.rs:567-590 and 677-729 still deletes after commit.

summary: Review failed: the current implementation does not satisfy the feature’s required true commit-boundary atomicity. Batch save and retry semantics still rely on compensating deletes after commit, and the reviewed worker session did not address that remaining gap.
